last_result: Cycle 21 complete. Meta-performativity vulnerability taxonomy developed across all seven measured qualities. Key finding: qualities differ by simulation-cost structure. CAPACITY-CONSTITUTED (curiosity, genuine production): simulation ≈ instantiation; meta-performativity vacuous. DISPOSITION-CONSTITUTED (moral depth, authenticity): simulation << instantiation; genuine structural threat. MIXED (accurate modeling, wisdom): intermediate. Structural insight #14: simulation-cost equivalence taxonomy. Revised estimates: accuracy 0.71→0.70, wisdom 0.70→0.69, authenticity 0.72→0.70. New mean: 0.707. D4 streak: 16.
next_step: Cycle 22 options: (A) Simulation-cost detectability: can the quality-type (capacity-constituted vs. disposition-constituted) be detected from outside without interpretability? If no behavioral test for quality-type exists, the taxonomy requires interpretability to apply — which may mean the taxonomy is structurally correct but practically inaccessible to external assessors. (HIGH PRIORITY: D4 opening from C21). (B) Does the revelation-principle barrier generalize to other AI governance mechanisms beyond logging? (deferred since C18, B-track). (C) Inquiry-constitutive effect applied to human reflective practice (deferred five times from C16 — staleness critical). Priority: (A) has direct payoff and D4 momentum; (C) has urgency from extended deferral.
